signals.name: Aux 12V battery age
signals.description: Confirmed responding on 726. Displays raw days converted to hours (A*24).

signals.name: Battery age raw counter
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Raw age counter; likely days. Latest test showed 153.

signals.name: Aux 12V battery state of charge
signals.description: Confirmed responding on 726. Raw percent-style value.

signals.name: Battery charge 4028 div255 compare
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula A*100/255; latest test showed about 32–33%. Kept as secondary battery charge value.

signals.name: Aux 12V battery voltage
signals.description: Confirmed responding on 726. Formula A/20 + 6.

signals.name: Aux 12V battery current
signals.description: Working on 726. Formula A-127; sign direction still needs confirmation. Unit aligned to Pelican schema: amps.

signals.name: Alternator current output
signals.description: Working/plausible. Unit aligned to Pelican schema: amps.

signals.name: Grille shutter duty cycle
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula A*100/255.

signals.name: Ambient air temperature sensor voltage
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val * 0.0048875855.

signals.name: A/C refrigerant pressure
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Raw signed kPa; sanity check units/value.

signals.name: A/C compressor pressure sensor voltage
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val / 1024.

signals.name: Ambient air temperature
signals.description: Working; close to dash ambient.

signals.name: Accelerator pedal position
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Showed 0–4% during testing. Used as the practical throttle-position connectable on this diesel because generic throttle-position candidates were rejected.

signals.name: Distance with MIL
signals.description: Confirmed responding.

signals.name: Warm-ups since DTC clear
signals.description: Confirmed responding.

signals.name: Distance since DTC clear
signals.description: Working diagnostic distance counter. Added as distance since DTCs cleared connectable.

signals.name: AdBlue tank level
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Showed around 17 L after recent fill; plausible against approx 18.5 L tank. Best AdBlue quantity candidate so far.

signals.name: AdBlue tank temperature
signals.description: Confirmed responding. AdBlue tank temperature.

signals.name: DPF / exhaust filter fullness
signals.description: Everest-confirmed DPF fullness / soot-load estimate using raw/100 percent. It fell from 73.53% to 25.90% during a captured automatic regeneration and was independently validated by dashboard decline, F48B state changes, EGR reopening and the distance-since-regen reset. It tracked the dash closely early in the burn but diverged during the final stage, so it should not be described as the dashboard's exact internal percentage.

signals.name: TEST DPF fullness 0610 raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion for the confirmed production DPF fullness / soot-load estimate. The first word remained live throughout the captured regeneration and its third packet word was an exact duplicate in all 187 samples.

signals.name: TEST DPF secondary soot model 0610 raw
signals.description: Testing only. Raw second 16-bit word from 220610. It fell from roughly 1000-1100 before regeneration to 11 immediately before completion, then began rising slightly after completion. Behaviour is consistent with a secondary soot-mass or remaining-burnable-soot model, but identity and units are not confirmed.

signals.name: TEST DPF secondary soot model 0610 div100
signals.description: Testing only. Second 220610 word decoded raw/100 as an unknown scalar, not percent. It fell from approximately 10-11 before the captured regeneration to 0.11 immediately before completion and was the closest observed packet quantity to a near-zero completed-burn state.

signals.name: Distance since DPF regen
signals.description: Everest-confirmed. Formula raw / 10 km. Validated during captured regen completion: distance since regen reset from about 125.8 km to 0.5 km, then immediately counted upward again. Strong production signal.

signals.name: EGR open percentage
signals.description: Working.

signals.name: Exhaust manifold EGT pre-turbo
signals.description: Working/plausible pre-turbo exhaust-temperature reference using raw/32 °C. During the captured active regeneration every sampled response decoded to exactly 200 °C, so the signal is retained in production but should not yet be treated as a sensitive regeneration-state indicator.

signals.name: Engine load
signals.description: Working engine load percentage. Added as engine load connectable.

signals.name: Coolant temperature
signals.description: Working; matched built-in ECT.

signals.name: Engine speed
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val / 4.

signals.name: Engine run time
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Raw seconds.

signals.name: Actual engine percent torque
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula A-125.

signals.name: Engine reference torque
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Raw N m value.

signals.name: Intercooler air charge temperature
signals.description: Working charge-air / post-intercooler temperature candidate. Added as a custom connectable using intercoolerAirChargeTemperature.

signals.name: Cylinder head temperature sensor 2
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val / 64.

signals.name: Intake temperature fault status
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Showed 0; likely no intake temperature fault.

signals.name: Intake air temperature sensor voltage
signals.description: Responds but showed 0 V during test. Keep as low-confidence candidate; compare later.

signals.name: Intake air temperature
signals.description: Working.

signals.name: Engine oil temperature
signals.description: Working. Equation A-40. Added as engine oil temperature connectable.

signals.name: Manifold absolute pressure sensor voltage
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val * 0.0009765625.

signals.name: Wastegate duty cycle
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val*100/32768.

signals.name: Manifold pressure
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula A*10000/68947.

signals.name: Oil life remaining
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Latest test showed 47%. Preferred raw-percent oil life signal.

signals.name: Generic manifold absolute pressure
signals.description: Generic SAE OBD-II MAP value, confirmed responding on the Everest. Kept under Engine.Generic because it is not an Everest-specific Ford enhanced PID.

signals.name: TEST Generic MAP gauge boost 010B fixed offset
signals.description: Testing only. Generic MAP converted to approximate gauge boost using fixed sea-level offset A-101 kPa. Watch for 255 kPa absolute cap under high load.

signals.name: TEST Generic MAP gauge boost 010B psi fixed offset
signals.description: Testing only. Generic MAP converted to approximate gauge boost in psi using fixed sea-level offset. Formula approximately A*0.145038-14.65. Added so 010B can be compared directly against the F40B psi boost candidate. Watch for the 255 kPa absolute cap under high load.

signals.name: Generic mass air flow rate
signals.description: Generic SAE OBD-II mass air flow rate, confirmed responding on the Everest. Kept under Engine.Generic because it is not an Everest-specific Ford enhanced PID.

signals.name: Distance to empty displayed
signals.description: Confirmed against dash at 336 km. Formula raw/10. Added as fuel range connectable.

signals.name: Fuel remaining
signals.description: Working. Equation A*100/255.

signals.name: Fuel temperature
signals.description: Working.

signals.name: Low-pressure fuel pump commanded duty cycle
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val * 0.0030517578.

signals.name: High-pressure fuel rail sensor voltage
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val / 204.6.

signals.name: High-pressure fuel pressure desired
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val * 1.4503773969.

signals.name: Low-pressure fuel pressure desired
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula A*435/1000.

signals.name: Low-pressure fuel pressure actual
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val*10000/137892.

signals.name: Low-pressure fuel pressure sensor voltage
signals.description: Confirmed responding. Formula val / 1024.

signals.name: Generic fuel level
signals.description: Generic SAE OBD-II fuel level, confirmed responding on the Everest. Kept under Fuel.Generic because it is not an Everest-specific Ford enhanced PID. Not used as the active fuel-level connectable; Everest-specific F42F is preferred.

signals.name: Vehicle speed Ford extended raw
signals.description: Confirmed working Ford extended vehicle speed. Raw value reads about 4 km/h below dashboard; kept as raw speed connectable.

signals.name: Vehicle speed Ford extended corrected
signals.description: Ford extended vehicle speed with +4 km/h display correction to better match the dashboard. Use this as the practical speed connectable.

signals.name: Front right wheel speed
signals.description: Confirmed working Ford wheel speed PID. Promoted from testing. Confirmed Ford wheel-speed PID; kept as a parameter only, not a speed connectable.

signals.name: Front left wheel speed
signals.description: Confirmed working Ford wheel speed PID. Promoted from testing. Confirmed Ford wheel-speed PID; kept as a parameter only, not a speed connectable.

signals.name: Rear right wheel speed
signals.description: Confirmed working Ford wheel speed PID. Promoted from testing. Confirmed Ford wheel-speed PID; kept as a parameter only, not a speed connectable.

signals.name: Rear left wheel speed
signals.description: Confirmed working Ford wheel speed PID. Promoted from testing. Confirmed Ford wheel-speed PID; kept as a parameter only, not a speed connectable.

signals.name: Lateral angle
signals.description: Working Ford lateral-angle parameter. Observed about -1 deg; verify orientation on level ground.

signals.name: Lateral acceleration
signals.description: Working Ford lateral-acceleration parameter. Formula raw/50; observed live values around -0.14 to 0.4. Unit aligned to Pelican schema: gravity.

signals.name: Odometer
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 odometer using raw/10 km. The latest complete drive increased from 28,807.0 km to 28,826.4 km across a measured 19.4 km session, confirming correct incremental behaviour.

signals.name: Transmission fluid temperature
signals.description: Promoted to primary Everest transmission-fluid-temperature candidate after Pelican drive comparison. Same 7E1 221E1C TCM response as the 5/72 value; div16 warm-up and operating range looked more plausible against coolant, oil temperature, speed/load, and normal 10-speed behaviour. Validate against FORScan/factory scan data when available.

signals.name: Transmission fluid temperature compare
signals.description: Comparison/legacy transmission fluid temperature decode using 5/72 scaling. Retained beside the preferred div16 Everest transmission temperature for validation only; no suggestedMetric.

signals.name: Transmission gear commanded
signals.description: Working; tested 1 and 9.

signals.name: Transmission current gear
signals.description: Working current gear indicator from 7E1/221E1F. Primary current-gear connectable. Use alongside the alternate 7E0/221E12 current gear signal for comparison.

signals.name: Torque converter slip speed
signals.description: Confirmed working torque converter slip RPM.

signals.name: Transmission output shaft speed
signals.description: Confirmed working as driveline output shaft RPM. Not direct road speed without conversion.

signals.name: Transmission input shaft speed
signals.description: Confirmed working as input/turbine shaft RPM. Not direct road speed.

signals.name: TEST exhaust flow 019E raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion retained for the confirmed generic engine exhaust-flow signal. SAE Mode 01 PID 9E decodes as raw/5 kilograms per hour; earlier raw/20 testing looked numerically similar to MAF because it approximated a mass-flow unit conversion rather than the defined engineering scale.

signals.name: Generic engine exhaust flow rate
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 engine exhaust-flow rate using raw/5 kilograms per hour. Earlier validation produced 79 samples with strong correlation to generic MAF (r=0.959); the defined kg/h scaling explains why the former raw/20 scalar appeared close to a grams-per-second airflow value.

signals.name: TEST EGT11 F46A raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion retained after active-regeneration packet analysis proved the previous raw*0.1-40 °C decode was misaligned by a leading 0x03 support byte. Packet bytes B/C moved from 44 to 50 while byte D remained 40; engineering meaning remains unresolved.

signals.name: TEST exhaust packet F46D raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion retained for F46D. Active-regeneration analysis proved the previous first-word raw*0.1-40 °C decode included a leading 0x07 support byte and was invalid. Correctly aligned shifted BC/DE scouts are provided separately.

signals.name: TEST exhaust packet F46D shifted BC raw
signals.description: Testing only. Correctly aligned first live F46D word after the leading 0x07 support byte. It ranged 6422-7246 during active regeneration and correlated at r=0.9995 with shifted DE.

signals.name: TEST exhaust packet F46D shifted DE raw
signals.description: Testing only. Correctly aligned second live F46D word. It ranged 6425-7243 and stayed within a median 4.5 counts of shifted BC during active regeneration.

signals.name: TEST exhaust packet F46D shifted BC div32
signals.description: Testing only. Shifted F46D BC raw/32 temperature candidate. It produced approximately 200.7-226.4 °C during the captured regeneration and needs comparison against confirmed exhaust temperatures before promotion.

signals.name: TEST exhaust packet F46D shifted DE div32
signals.description: Testing only. Shifted F46D DE raw/32 temperature candidate. It produced approximately 200.8-226.3 °C and tracked shifted BC almost exactly during the captured regeneration.

signals.name: TEST exhaust packet F46D byte F raw
signals.description: Testing only. F46D state/reference byte following the paired shifted words. It moved from 65 to 70 during the captured active regeneration; exact meaning is unresolved.

signals.name: TEST exhaust multi-EGT F478 raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion retained for F478. Active-regeneration analysis proved the previous first-word raw*0.1-40 °C decode included a leading 0x07 support byte and was invalid. Correctly aligned shifted BC/DE scouts are provided separately; shifted FG duplicated DE exactly.

signals.name: TEST exhaust multi F478 shifted BC raw
signals.description: Testing only. Correctly aligned first F478 word after the leading 0x07 support byte. It ranged 3899-4085 during active regeneration.

signals.name: TEST exhaust multi F478 shifted DE raw
signals.description: Testing only. Correctly aligned second F478 word. It ranged 6462-6629; the following shifted FG word duplicated it exactly in all 11 samples and is intentionally not exposed.

signals.name: TEST exhaust multi F478 shifted BC div32
signals.description: Testing only. Shifted F478 BC raw/32 temperature candidate. It produced approximately 121.8-127.7 °C during active regeneration; sensor identity is unresolved.

signals.name: TEST exhaust multi F478 shifted DE div32
signals.description: Testing only. Shifted F478 DE raw/32 temperature candidate. It produced approximately 201.9-207.2 °C during active regeneration; sensor identity is unresolved.

signals.name: TEST TCC desired slip 1E35 raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion retained for the promoted TCC desired-slip signal. Raw 4092 corresponds to the observed 1023 rpm open/unlocked sentinel under the confirmed raw/4 decode.

signals.name: TCC desired slip
signals.description: Confirmed transmission torque-converter-clutch desired slip using raw/4 rpm. Captures showed 1023 rpm while the converter was open/unlocked, followed by low single-digit desired slip as TCC apply command increased; the validation drive showed 0-5 rpm during locked or near-locked highway operation. Treat 1023 rpm as an open/unlocked sentinel rather than literal controlled slip.

signals.name: TEST TCC apply command 1E3C raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion retained for the promoted TCC apply-command scalar. The engineering pressure scale remains unknown, so the production signal deliberately avoids pressure units.

signals.name: TCC apply command
signals.description: Confirmed torque-converter-clutch apply-command quantity. Multiple captures showed 0 with the 1023 rpm open-converter desired-slip sentinel, a strong rise during clutch application, and sustained non-zero values with 0-5 rpm desired slip during locked or near-locked driving. Exact hydraulic pressure scaling remains unknown, so the value is intentionally exposed as a scalar.

signals.name: Transmission current gear alt
signals.description: Promoted from TESTING.Transmission in v0.7.11. 7E0/221E12 matched the dash gear during live driving and changed at the same time as the dash gear display. Kept as an alternate current-gear signal because another working transmission current gear indicator already exists. Raw 70 is mapped as not in gear / unavailable based on stopped/end-state log evidence. Added scalar unit for Pelican schema alignment.

signals.name: Generic barometric pressure
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 barometric pressure. The latest database contained 16/16 valid responses at 100 kPa despite the old TESTING widget displaying blank, proving the ECU command and decode are valid. The fresh production ID also avoids retaining the stale testing widget association.

signals.name: TEST boost-control packet 0170 raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion for the confirmed SAE boost-control packet. The apparent first word includes the 0x07 support mask and is not itself a pressure value; correctly aligned production command/actual signals begin at byte B.

signals.name: TEST boost packet 0170 shifted BC raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for commanded boost pressure A. SAE Mode 01 PID 70 defines this shifted BC field as raw/32 kPa; the decoded production signal is provided alongside it.

signals.name: TEST boost packet 0170 shifted DE raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for actual boost pressure A. SAE Mode 01 PID 70 defines this shifted DE field as raw/32 kPa; synchronized comparisons tracked generic MAP closely while providing 16-bit range.

signals.name: TEST boost-control packet 0170 final raw word
signals.description: Testing raw companion spanning the final I/J bytes. Observed whole-word values 5, 6 and 10 are normal combinations of boost-control status bits, not regeneration states. Production boost-control status A is decoded separately from the low two bits of byte J.

signals.name: Generic commanded boost pressure A
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 commanded turbocharger/supercharger A absolute pressure using shifted BC raw/32 kPa. Latest captures ranged from approximately atmospheric pressure at idle to substantially higher commanded pressure under load.

signals.name: Generic actual boost pressure A
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 actual turbocharger/supercharger A absolute pressure using shifted DE raw/32 kPa. Time-near comparisons tracked generic MAP closely while avoiding the 8-bit 255 kPa ceiling of PID 010B.

signals.name: Generic boost pressure A control status
signals.description: SAE Mode 01 boost-pressure A control state decoded from the low two bits of the final packet byte. Latest normal driving demonstrated ordinary open-loop and closed-loop transitions; it is not a DPF-regeneration indicator.

signals.name: TEST Ford boost-control mirror F470 raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw first-word companion for Ford DID F470. The response mirrors the SAE 0170 boost-control packet structure; this word includes the support byte and must not be interpreted as a VGT percentage.

signals.name: TEST Ford boost-control mirror F470 shifted BC raw
signals.description: Testing raw first pressure field from the Ford F470 mirror. Its behaviour matches commanded boost pressure using raw/32 kPa, but the standardized 0170 production signal is preferred to avoid duplicate production widgets.

signals.name: TEST Ford boost-control mirror F470 shifted DE raw
signals.description: Testing raw second pressure field from the Ford F470 mirror. Its behaviour matches actual boost pressure using raw/32 kPa; retain temporarily for synchronized equivalence testing against standardized 0170.

signals.name: TEST Ford boost-control mirror F470 final raw word
signals.description: Testing final raw word from the Ford F470 boost-control mirror. Values 5, 6 and 10 occurred during ordinary non-regen driving and encode open/closed-loop status combinations; prior regeneration-specific wording was incorrect.

signals.name: TEST VGT-control packet F471 raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw first-word companion for Ford DID F471. The word combines the 0x07 support mask with the first VGT position byte; use the correctly aligned raw and production fields instead of decoding this word directly.

signals.name: TEST VGT F471 commanded-position byte B raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for commanded VGT A position. Across 81 combined responses it moved coherently with byte C; the production view applies the standard raw*100/255 percent scaling.

signals.name: TEST VGT F471 actual-position byte C raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for actual VGT A position. Across 81 combined responses it tracked commanded byte B closely while retaining plausible transient separation; the production view applies raw*100/255 percent.

signals.name: TEST VGT F471 final state byte raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for the final VGT-control state byte. Values 1 and 2 occurred during normal driving and correspond to open-loop and closed-loop control; this is not a regeneration state.

signals.name: VGT commanded position
signals.description: Confirmed commanded variable-geometry-turbo A position from Ford DID F471 using raw*100/255 percent. The packet matches the SAE VGT-control layout and produced coherent commanded/actual behaviour across 81 combined responses.

signals.name: VGT actual position
signals.description: Confirmed actual variable-geometry-turbo A position from Ford DID F471 using raw*100/255 percent. It tracked commanded position closely while retaining plausible control transients under changing load.

signals.name: VGT control status
signals.description: Variable-geometry-turbo A control state decoded from the low two bits of Ford DID F471's final byte. Normal driving captured both open-loop and closed-loop operation.

signals.name: TEST F47A turbo packet raw AB
signals.description: Testing only. Raw first-word companion for F47A. The previous rpm candidate stayed fixed and has been removed; keep raw AB only as a low-confidence scout because later packet bytes may still contain useful data if byte offsets become available. Companion byte-offset scouts were added in v0.7.16 using Pelican fmt.bix. Dedicated BIX packet-scout group; retained because this PID previously responded but the first-word decode was frozen, low-value, or not fully decoded.

signals.name: TEST turbo packet F47A shifted DE raw
signals.description: Testing only. First retained live F47A shifted field. Across 71 responses it ranged 0-3340 and correlated strongly with shifted FG (r=0.976). Avoid turbo-speed or RPM wording until identified.

signals.name: TEST turbo packet F47A shifted FG raw
signals.description: Testing only. Second retained live F47A shifted field. Across 71 responses it ranged 0-1560 and tracked shifted DE strongly. Misaligned overlapping fields and the all-zero shifted BC scout were removed.

signals.name: Generic fuel rate
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 fuel-rate signal. Raw/20 produced plausible L/h-style values across idle, cruise and load, returned valid data on every request in the validation drive, and correlated almost perfectly with the independent 019D fuel-rate packet (r=0.99999). Pelican has no volume-flow unit, so the decoded litres-per-hour value is intentionally exposed as scalar.

signals.name: TEST fuel rate alt 019D raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion for the confirmed SAE alternate engine fuel-rate field. It matched the vehicle-rate word in all 15 normal-drive samples but diverged during captured active regeneration; the decoded production signal uses raw/50 grams per second.

signals.name: TEST fuel rate alt 019D raw CD
signals.description: Testing raw companion for the confirmed SAE vehicle fuel-rate field. It matched the engine-rate word in all 15 normal-drive samples but rose from 44 to 69 while the engine word remained 44 during active regeneration, confirming that both fields must be retained.

signals.name: Generic engine fuel rate (alternate)
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 alternate engine fuel rate using raw/50 grams per second. It matched vehicle fuel rate in all 15 latest normal-drive samples and remained independently useful when the two rates diverged during active regeneration.

signals.name: Generic vehicle fuel rate
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 vehicle fuel rate using raw/50 grams per second. During captured active regeneration it exceeded the alternate engine-rate word, making it a useful view of total vehicle fuel consumption during emissions-control operation.

signals.name: TEST fuel-pressure packet 016D raw AB
signals.description: Testing raw companion for the confirmed SAE fuel-pressure-control packet. The apparent first word includes the 0x07 support mask and is not a rail-pressure measurement.

signals.name: TEST fuel rail packet 016D shifted BC raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for commanded fuel rail pressure A. Across 27 combined samples it tracked the actual field at r=0.99958; the production signal applies the defined raw*10 kPa scaling.

signals.name: TEST fuel rail packet 016D shifted DE raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for actual fuel rail pressure A. Across 27 combined samples the command/actual median absolute difference was approximately 0.25 MPa; the production signal applies raw*10 kPa.

signals.name: TEST fuel rail packet 016D byte F raw
signals.description: Testing raw companion for fuel rail temperature A. The defined raw-40 °C decode converts observed bytes 55-73 into a plausible 15-33 °C range.

signals.name: Generic commanded fuel rail pressure A
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 commanded fuel rail pressure A using shifted BC raw*10 kPa. Latest captures ranged from approximately 30 MPa at light/idle operation to approximately 203 MPa under load.

signals.name: Generic fuel rail pressure A
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 actual fuel rail pressure A using shifted DE raw*10 kPa. It followed commanded pressure almost perfectly across 27 combined samples and produced plausible common-rail diesel pressures.

signals.name: Generic fuel rail temperature A
signals.description: Confirmed SAE Mode 01 fuel rail temperature A using byte F raw-40 °C. Observed values of 15-33 °C were plausible for ambient-to-warmed fuel during the retained drives.

signals.name: Driver demand torque
signals.description: Promoted from testing. SAE Mode 01 PID 61. Confirmed live: rises with accelerator/load demand and can show 0% while coasting.

signals.name: Actual engine torque
signals.description: Promoted from testing. SAE Mode 01 PID 62. Confirmed live: tracks delivered engine torque/load and can show 0% while coasting.

signals.name: Engine reference torque
signals.description: Promoted from testing. SAE Mode 01 PID 63. Confirmed stable around 500 N m; useful reference value for torque percent interpretation.

signals.name: TEST EGR command actual packet 0169 raw AB
signals.description: Testing only. Raw companion for SAE/Ranger EGR command/actual packet 0169. Latest captures showed live movement around raw 1792-1846; keep as alive scout for correlation against EGR open percentage, RPM and load. Companion byte-offset scouts were added in v0.7.16 using Pelican fmt.bix. Dedicated BIX packet-scout group; retained because this PID previously responded but the first-word decode was frozen, low-value, or not fully decoded.

signals.name: TEST EGR command actual packet 0169 cd raw16
signals.description: Testing only. Pelican bix-based byte-offset scout for EGR command actual packet 0169. Added to inspect later packet words without relying only on the first A/B word. Dedicated BIX packet-scout group; retained because this PID previously responded but the first-word decode was frozen, low-value, or not fully decoded.

signals.name: TEST EGR command actual packet 0169 ef raw16
signals.description: Testing only. Pelican bix-based byte-offset scout for EGR command actual packet 0169. Added to inspect later packet words without relying only on the first A/B word. Dedicated BIX packet-scout group; retained because this PID previously responded but the first-word decode was frozen, low-value, or not fully decoded.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 byte B raw
signals.description: Testing only. First useful byte after the stable 0x07 packet prefix. Compare with EGR open percentage, load, RPM, fuel and regen state.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 byte B percent
signals.description: Testing only. Byte B scaled as A*100/255 after the packet prefix. Candidate commanded or actual EGR percentage.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 byte C raw
signals.description: Testing only. Second useful byte after the packet prefix. Retained raw for command/actual/error comparison.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 byte C percent
signals.description: Testing only. Byte C scaled as A*100/255. Compare with byte B percent and production EGR open percentage.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 byte D raw
signals.description: Testing only. Third useful byte after the packet prefix. May be EGR error, limit, status or another requested/actual value.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 byte D percent
signals.description: Testing only. Byte D scaled as A*100/255 for comparison with bytes B and C.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 shifted BC raw
signals.description: Testing only. Shifted 16-bit BC field using bix 8. Added to preserve paired-field analysis alongside individual byte scouts.

signals.name: TEST EGR packet 0169 shifted CD raw
signals.description: Testing only. Shifted overlapping 16-bit CD field using bix 16. Compare against the individual B/C/D bytes before assigning meaning.

signals.name: TEST DPF status F48B byte C raw
signals.description: Testing only. F48B byte C tracked the production 220610 soot/fullness estimate extremely closely during active regeneration (r=0.994), declining from 218 to 166 before the completion transition and then to 112. It appears to be another scaled soot/load quantity rather than a simple state flag.

signals.name: TEST DPF status F48B byte E raw
signals.description: Testing only. F48B byte E formed a strong phase signature: 232-233 during earlier normal driving, 1 throughout the captured active regeneration, and 180 immediately after completion. Keep for a second regen comparison.

signals.name: TEST DPF status F48B byte G raw
signals.description: Testing only. F48B byte G formed a distinct phase signature: 131 during earlier normal driving, 139 throughout the captured active regeneration, and 107 immediately after completion. It is not yet decoded as an individual bitfield.

signals.name: TEST DPF status F48B byte D raw
signals.description: Testing only. Best active-regeneration state candidate so far. Byte D was 0 during previous normal driving, 1 throughout the captured active burn, and returned to 0 approximately 41 seconds before distance-since-regen reset to zero. Requires one more confirmed regeneration before promotion.

signals.name: TEST DPF active regen F48B byte D
signals.description: Testing-only on/off interpretation of F48B byte D. Current evidence supports 0=not active and 1=active: the value changed back to 0 before EGR reopened and distance-since-regen reset. Do not promote until repeated in another confirmed burn.
